name: Biscotti All'anice
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Combine the eggs, anise extract, sugar, and salt in a mixing bowl or the bowl of an electric mixer. Whisk with a hand mixer set at high speed or in a heavy-duty mixer fitted with the whisk attachment until the mixture is very light and increased in volume, about 6-7 minutes.
3. While whipping the egg mixture, sift together the flour, cornstarch, and baking powder.
4. Remove the whipped eggs from the mixer and fold them into the dry ingredients three times using a rubber spatula. The batter will become stiff.
5. Pipe the batter onto a jelly roll pan lined with parchment paper or buttered wax paper in two logs measuring about 1 1/2 inches wide and the length of the pan.
6. Bake for 20 minutes until well risen and golden.
7. Remove from oven and place logs on a cutting board to cool for 10 minutes.
8. Slice diagonally into 1/2-inch intervals using a sharp, serrated knife.
9. Place the biscotti cut side down on the pan and return them to the oven for an additional 10-15 minutes until lightly colored on the cut surfaces.
10. Cool on pans and store in a tin between layers of wax paper. Makes 48 cookies.
